steps.text: You spot something up ahead:"

Among the headstones, a half-buried figure hunches over a flat-topped grave marker. Three dice on the stone in front of it. It doesn't have eyes anymore, but the empty sockets turn toward you when you approach.

"Sit," it says, in a voice like air through old stone. "One game of Wandering Dice. That is all I ask."

on_win.text:
Your hand takes it. He holds still for a moment, then counts out the coins and sets them in front of you.

"...Well played." He reaches behind the headstone and sets a die on the stone between you. Small, carved from bone, every face the same mark. "Keep it. Something to remember the game by."

on_tie.text:
A dead match. He divides the pot exactly and sets your half in front of you without a word. Then he reaches behind the headstone and places a small die on the stone between you. Every face carved with the same sign.

"Even hands. Take this anyway."

on_fold.text:
You set your dice down. He pulls the pot to his side with careful hands.

"Wise, perhaps." He doesn't look up from the stone. "Come back when you are ready."

on_lose.text:
He sweeps the coins to his side slowly. Then reaches behind the headstone and sets a small bone die on the grave marker between you. Every face carved with the same sign.

"The game leaves something behind," he says. "It always does."

leave.text:
You back away from the grave. The skeleton watches you go, then looks back down at its dice.

It doesn't call after you.

steps.text: A wide crevice splits the ground ahead — deeper than it looks, a faint light coming from the bottom.

You crouch and look down...
[wait=0.1]...[wait=0.1]
Nothing down there worth the trouble. Just loose stone and silence.
Old coins are wedged in the stone near the lip — scattered, as though dropped in a hurry.
You pry them out. [color=yellow]20G[/color].

A hand seizes your ankle from below.

A Zombie — buried just under the rim, skin grey and dry.

on_victory.text: The grip goes slack. You pull away and steady yourself.

steps.text: Something glints far down in the crack. You fish it out with care.
A [color=green]Potion[/color], still sealed.
A worn pack is wedged into a rocky ledge just below the rim. You can see it clearly — whoever left it didn't do so on purpose.

You lower yourself along the face and hook the pack with steady hands.

Something valuable inside — worth the trouble.
You found a [color=yellow]Lifespark Crystal[/color].
Your footing slips. You catch yourself on the rim, but the jolt rattles you hard.
You took [color=red]20 damage[/color] in the fall.

leave.text: Not worth the risk. You step back from the edge.

steps.text:
Half-buried in dust, an old campfire sits in a shallow depression between cracked stones.

Someone camped here before you. The bedroll is still spread, the pack left open. They either moved on quickly or didn't move on at all.

rest.text:
You settle in and close your eyes.

Sleep comes reluctantly, broken by the plateau's quiet sounds — wind, dry earth, something distant.

You wake stiff, but steadier.

leave.text: You leave the camp undisturbed and press on.

steps.text: The tunnel ends in a collapse — ceiling and walls giving way into a wall of loose stone and old timber.

dig.text: You work through the rubble — stone by stone, careful not to bring more down.

steps.text: Behind the collapse, a small alcove. Shelved, like a storage niche — whatever it was for, someone stocked it.
You found a [color=red]Bomb[/color].
You found an [color=teal]Ether[/color].
You dig until your arms ache. Nothing back there — just more stone, and more collapse beyond.
Half-buried in the cracked earth: a leather pack, a pair of boots, and what's left of whoever wore them.

search.text:
Inside the pack: a folded journal, the later pages stiff with dried water.

You can still make out a few entries:

[color=gray]"...the plateau is as desolate as they said. Found the mausoleum."[/color]
[color=gray]"Descended into the catacombs this morning. There are symbols I haven't seen before — not funerary, not ceremonial. Something older."[/color]

[color=gray]"Reached a circular chamber. The floor pulses. I don't think this place was a tomb."[/color]

The last entry trails off into nothing.

steps.text: Beneath the journal: a [color=green]Potion[/color], still good.
Beneath the journal: an [color=teal]Ether[/color], intact.

leave.text: You leave them where they fell.
